channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump lays wreath at Tomb of the Unknown Soldier ahead of inauguration
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump participated in a wreath-laying ceremony at the Tomb of the Unknown Soldier on Sunday, the eve of his return to the White House.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden urges Americans to 'keep the faith' as he spends final full day as president in South Carolina
item.description: NORTH CHARLESTON, S.C. (AP) — Joe Biden spent his final full day as president Sunday in South Carolina, urging Americans to “keep the faith in a better day to come” and reflecting on the influence of both the civil rights movement and the state itself in his political trajectory.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE and REBECCA SANTANA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Lindsey Graham defends Kash Patel in heated exchange with CBS host
item.description: Sen. Lindsey Graham, R-S.C., not only defends President-elect Donald Trump's pick for FBI director, but explains why he supports him while speaking on CBS' "Face the Nation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump's inauguration will be first attended by foreign leaders
item.description: For the first time in U.S. history, a president-elect will welcome foreign leaders for one of the most American political traditions — the peaceful transfer of power.
item.dc:creator: ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Republicans set to give Trump big immigration win
item.description: Republicans are on the verge of giving President-elect Trump a big win on immigration to kick off his second term — and doing it with the help of Democrats while dividing the minority party on the hot-button issue.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: What to watch at Trump's inauguration: Big Tech CEOs, Carrie Underwood and foreign leaders
item.description: Donald Trump's inauguration as the country’s 47th president was expected to be an extravagant break from tradition — before it got moved indoors due to cold weather. There will still be well-known performers, influential billionaires as guests and foreign heads of state.
item.dc:creator: ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump meets with GOP senators as supporters cheer his return on eve of inauguration
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump consulted privately with Republican senators before heading off to a series of events designed to celebrate his return to power and the “Make America Great Again” movement.
item.dc:creator: Will Weissert, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Northern highlights: Alaska's energy, security policies are the guide feds need amid transition, group says
item.description: Future 49, with proponents from civilian pilots to Gov. Mike Dunleavy, is primed to launch to promote Alaskan issues and its energy, economic and national security benefits to the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Youngkin orders flags to be raised for Trump's inauguration amid mourning period for Jimmy Carter's death
item.description: Virginia Gov. Glenn Youngkin ordered to raise flags temporarily for President-elect Donald Trump's inauguration on Monday, despite the mourning period to honor Jimmy Carter.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Washington governor orders flags at full-staff for Trump inauguration despite mourning period for Jimmy Carter
item.description: Washington Gov. Bob Ferguson has ordered flags to be raised on Monday for President-elect Donald Trump's inauguration, despite the 30-day mourning period for Jimmy Carter's death.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Outgoing Interior Secretary Deb Haaland hands off closer ties with Indian Country
item.description: Deb Haaland, the country's first Indigenous cabinet secretary, used her term at the Interior Department to make what activists say is irreversible impact in recognizing the painful history of the government's treatment Native Americans.
item.dc:creator: Kirk Siegler, MPR News

item.title: Civil rights leaders and King family mark MLK Day as a special call to action as Trump takes office
item.description: Events honoring Martin Luther King Jr.’s legacy and advocating for his vision of a just, nonviolent society will occur the same day as Donald Trump's second presidential inauguration.
item.dc:creator: Matt Brown, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Biden's departing UN envoy says US rivals will fill the vacuum if Trump abandons global leadership
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The outgoing U.S. ambassador to the United Nations says she watched America’s leadership diminish in the world during Donald Trump’s first presidency and China fill the vacuum.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump's family circle has a different look as he returns to the White House
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — When Donald Trump returns to the White House on Monday, his family circle will look a little different than it did when he first arrived eight years ago. His youngest son, Barron, was in fifth grade back then.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE and ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mass deportation raids could start in Chicago as soon as Tuesday: Report
item.description: Mass deportation raids threatened in Chicago could start as soon as Tuesday, according to a report from the Wall Street Journal.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Illers, NewsNation

item.title: Guard troops will wear a special patch at Trump's inauguration to make it clear they aren't police
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — When thousands of National Guard forces and law enforcement officers locked down Washington during racial protests and the Jan. 6 riot four years ago, the blur of camouflage and helmets made it nearly impossible to tell the difference between cops and troops.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Free upgrades and pardon requests: What it's like to share a name with a president
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — There’s not only one Donald Trump in the United States. Nor is there only one William J. Clinton. Plenty of people share names with famous presidents. Donald Trump, a veteran and resident of Alabama, said because of his name he often is gifted things for free. William J.
item.dc:creator: MIKE PESOLI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Merrick Garland exits with his record under scrutiny and the Justice Department bracing for upheaval
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — During hearings on Merrick Garland's nomination to be President Joe Biden's attorney general, the longtime federal appeals court judge told senators in 2021 that he hoped to “turn down the volume” on the public discourse about the Justice Department and return to the days when the
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, ERIC TUCKER and COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The inauguration is moving indoors. Here's what to know about the changed plans
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Sub-freezing temperatures forecast for Monday in Washington are prompting President-elect Donald Trump to take the oath of office from inside the Capitol Rotunda. Almost everyone visiting Washington won’t be able to see it in person.
item.dc:creator: MEG KINNARD and THOMAS BEAUMONT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Minnesota Supreme Court sides with GOP, cancels special election key to House control
item.description: In late December, DFL Gov. Tim Walz set a special election for Jan. 28. But the Supreme Court sided with Republicans who said Walz acted too quickly in setting the date and postponed the election indefinitely.
item.dc:creator: Matt Sepic and Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Live updates: Noem pledges to eliminate CBP One app; Senate advances Laken Riley Act
item.description: South Dakota Gov. Kristi Noem (R), President-elect Trump's pick to head the Department of Homeland Security (DHS), is appearing for her confirmation hearing before a Senate panel Friday. The purview of DHS involves immigration and federal emergency management — two hot-button topics.
item.dc:creator: The Hill staff, NewsNation

item.title: NYC Mayor Eric Adams to meet President-elect Trump in Florida meeting
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – New York City Mayor Eric Adams will be meeting with President-elect Donald Trump on Friday, a spokesperson for the mayor has confirmed. Adams is traveling to Palm Beach, Florida, to discuss the New Yorker’s priorities.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Euzarraga, PIX 11

item.title: Official portraits of Donald Trump, JD Vance are released
item.description: The photos were revealed on Trump's website in a post titled "Official Portraits Released — And They Go Hard ????" and show Trump with his signature near-scowl and Vance with arms crossed, smiling.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, NewsNation

item.title: No taxes on tips comes closer to reality with Cortez Masto, Rosen push
item.description: No taxes on tips is becoming a closer reality for millions of workers after Democratic Nevada Sens. Catherine Cortez Masto and Jacky Rosen and other senators reintroduced the proposal Thursday, her office said.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Rubio vows to place US interests 'above all else' as Trump's top diplomat
item.description: Florida Sen. Marco Rubio promised on Wednesday to implement President-elect Donald Trump’s “America First” vision as secretary of state, vowing in his confirmation hearing that the incoming administration will forge a new path by placing American interests “above all else.
item.dc:creator: FARNOUSH AMIRI and MATTHEW LEE Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: NY plan would make community college free: Who qualifies
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Gov. Kathy Hochul on Tuesday announced a plan to make community college free for adults pursuing in-demand careers.
item.dc:creator: Finn Hoogensen, PIX 11

item.title: Nevada Gov. Joe Lombardo raises $3.4 million in 2024 for re-election bid
item.description: Republican Gov. Joe Lombardo is off to a strong start in building campaign funds, raising about $3.4 million over the past year, according to mandatory campaign filing reports.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News
